title: Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka

'''Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka''' was an [[Ewok]] [[Gender|female]] who resided in [[Bright Tree Village]] on the [[Endor|Forest Moon of Endor]]. She was the daughter of the village [[chief]], [[Chirpa]], and she held the title of [[prince]]ss. In [[4 ABY]], Kneesaa participated in the [[Battle of Endor]], helping the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] in their fight against the [[Galactic Empire]]. Kneesaa's bravery and leadership led her to succeed her father and become the village's first female chief.

==Biography==
===Fighting the Empire===
[[Princess]] Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka<ref name="Jaxxon Annual 2023" /> was a [[Gender|female]] [[Ewok]] warrior from the [[forest]] [[moon]] of [[Endor]]. She was the daughter of [[Chirpa]], [[chief]] of the [[Bright Tree Village]] [[Bright Tree tribe|tribe]], and his [[Marriage|wife]] [[Ra-Lee]].<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars NE" /> Together with her friends [[Wicket Wystri Warrick|Wicket W. Warrick]], [[Teebo]], [[Paploo]], [[Weechee (Ewok)|Weechee]], and [[Widdle Warrick]], she had an adventurous [[Youngling|childhood]].<ref name="Character Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]''</ref>

[[File:LeiaEwoksEndor-SWGoH.png|250px|thumb|left|Kneesa fought alongside the Rebels against the Empire.]]
By the [[Standard year|year]] [[4 ABY]],<ref name="Galactic Atlas">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''</ref> the tyrannical [[Galactic Empire]] had set foot on Kneesaa's [[homeworld]] and established a [[Deflector shield generator|shield generator]] [[Death Star's shield generator|complex]] to protect its newest weapon of mass destruction, the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|second Death Star]], that was being built in orbit of the moon. Eventually, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] against the Empire sent a [[Endor strike team|strike team]] to Endor to destroy the shield generator, whose members befriended the Ewoks of Bright Tree Village and convinced them to join the fight so the [[Rebel Alliance Navy|Rebel Fleet]] could blow up the station and finally kill [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]] while he was onboard.<ref name="Episode VI">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Princess Kneesaa participated in the ensuing [[Battle of Endor]], helping rebel [[Special Forces Trackers Unit|pathfinder]] [[Kes Dameron]] to coordinate complicated tactical maneuvers. They stayed with each other throughout the battle, communicating through verbal stresses and [[Sign language|gestures]]. Instead of attending the post victory celebration, Kneesaa and Dameron [[Toast (ritual)|toasted]] their success together with a bottle of [[sunberry wine]].<ref name="On the Front Lines" />

===Facing the Gorax===
{{Quote|What do you mean, you need to reset your traps?|Leia Organa, to the agitated Kneesaa and Wicket|<ref name="Traps and Tribulations">{{FOD|Traps and Tribulations}}</ref>}}
[[File:Gorax-FOD.png|thumb|right|200px|Kneesaa is confronted by a Gorax]]
Shortly after the victory, a gigantic, predatory [[Gorax]] ventured out into the grounds of the Bright tree tribe. The distressed princess Kneesaa and her friend Wicket enlisted the help of the Rebel heroes [[Jedi Knight]] and [[Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker]] and his sister who is also Wicket's friend [[General]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] to reset the tribe's Gorax traps, which had been re-purposed for taking out the Imperial [[All Terrain Scout Transport]]s during the Battle of Endor. Once they arrived at the location of the traps, the group immediately encountered the Gorax, and were forced to scatter. As the creature pushed Skywalker and Organa into a corner, Kneesaa and her friend managed to climb up a tree where one of the log traps was installed, and called Organa for help. Kneesaa threw the general her [[spear]], which she used to cut a rope while Skywalker faced the giant, creating a diversion. Finally, Skywalker lured the Gorax right in front of the trap, and lend Organa [[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber|his]] [[lightsaber]]. With the rope cut at last, the giant trunk swung into the predator, throwing it into a nearby [[swamp]] where it fell unsconscious. As Skywalker thanked Organa and retrieved his lightsaber, Princess Kneesaa and Wicket cheered, breaking into a happy dance.<ref name="Traps and Tribulations" />

===Flying with the ''Ghost''===
{{Quote|Ooh, ya!|Kneesaa cheering as she blasts TIE fighters|<ref name="Chopper and Friends">{{FOD|Chopper and Friends}}</ref>}}
[[File:FODChopperandFriends.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Kneesaa the gunner]]
In the immediate aftermath of the Battle of Endor, Kneesaa and [[Tokkat]] also befriended [[C1-10P|Chopper]], an old [[astromech droid]] that belonged to the Skywalker twins' close friend Rebel general [[Hera Syndulla]], and the Ewoks attempted to teach the droid how to use a spear. However, the training session was interrupted when Syndulla came to inform the [[droid]] that the [[comm/scan processor]] of her [[starship]], the ''[[Ghost]]'', had picked up a couple stray Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter|TIE fighters]] heading for the Ewoks' village. Because they did not have time to gather any more crew members, Syndulla thought that only she and Chopper would have to do. The droid, however, brought Princess Kneesaa and her Tokkat aboard without his owner's knowledge. Although the general was disatisfied at first, Kneesaa and the other Ewok did wonders handling the ship's turrets, despite their small stature. Having proved their mettle in Syndulla's eyes, Kneesaa and her friend chattered excitedly as the Ghost took them home.<ref name="Chopper and Friends" /> Kneesaa later attended the [[wedding of Han Solo and Leia Organa]].<ref name="Someone Who Loves You">''[[Someone Who Loves You]]''</ref>

===Ewok Civil War===
At some point later, her father disappeared during a [[Dulok]] raid, an [[Ewok Civil War]] broke out between the supporters of [[Graak (lawbreaker)|Graak]] and the supporters of Kneesaa over who had a right lead the Bright Tree Village. In fact Chirpa had been kidnapped by the [[Children of Vulgarr]], a Dulok faction aided by Graak and his brother [[Gruekk]], and Graak was only alleging Chirpa had abandoned his people in order to take control of the village. After Kneesaa and chief [[shaman]] [[Logray]] requested help, a [[New Republic]] [[Diplomatic mission to Endor|diplomatic mission]] consisting of [[captain]] [[Jaxxon T. Tumperakki]] and the [[protocol droid]] [[C-3PO]] were sent by [[senator]] Organa to resolve the conflict. Jaxxon was eventually able to discover the truth, free Chripa, and end the usurpers' plot.<ref name="Jaxxon">''[[Hyperspace Stories Annual—Jaxxon 2023]]''</ref>

Kneesaa's great bravery and leadership skills eventually allowed her to succeed her father as chief of [[Bright Tree Village]], becoming the tribe's first female chief.<ref name="Absolutely Everything You Need to Know" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kneesaa first appeared in the [[2015]] non-[[canon]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Disney Infinity 3.0]]''.<ref name="Infinity">''[[Disney Infinity 3.0]]''</ref> She was later introduced in canon in the 2015 reference book ''[[Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know]]''.<ref name="Absolutely Everything You Need to Know" /> Kneesa made her first canon appearance in the [[Forces of Destiny: Season Two|second season]] of the animated micro-series ''[[Star Wars Forces of Destiny]]'',<ref name="Traps and Tribulations" /><ref name="Chopper and Friends" /> she was voiced by [[Shelby Young]].<ref name="Shelby Young">{{Twitter|shelby_young|status/992591268256497664|[[Shelby Young]]|quote=Excited to say that along with #Leia, I also had the opportunity to voice #Kneesaa in this batch of #ForcesofDestiny episodes!!! A very happy #MayTheFourth indeed|archivefile=File:Shelby young.png}}</ref> Kneesaa was originally introduced in ''[[Star Wars Legends]]'' in the [[1984]] book ''[[How the Ewoks Saved the Trees: An Old Ewok Legend]]'', written by [[James Howe]].<ref name="Saved the Trees">''[[How the Ewoks Saved the Trees: An Old Ewok Legend]]''</ref>
